properties.otherAdvice: Changed traffic conditions, including lane closures and reduced speed limits, will be in place at various times along Mamre Rd, and other nearby roads, as part of the Mamre Rd upgrade project.
properties.displayName: CHANGED TRAFFIC CONDITIONS Mamre Road upgrade

properties.otherAdvice: The road has been reduced to a single lane in each direction 24/7 until approximately September 2026 for work associated with the M6 Stage 1 project.

Changes to traffic conditions will be in place on the Sydney Harbour Bridge at various times while the bridge and other infrastructure are being upgraded and maintained.Â These changes include a combination of lane closures and speed reductions.

At least one lane will be maintained in each direction during work hours.

Access to Cahill Exp over Circular Quay southbound may not be available at times. Motorists can detour via the city using Grosvenor St, Bridge St, and Macquarie St for access to the Eastern Suburbs and airport.
properties.displayName: CHANGED TRAFFIC CONDITIONS Sydney Harbour Bridge

properties.otherAdvice: Snowy 2.0 construction vehicles only will be allowed access.
properties.displayName: CHANGED TRAFFIC CONDITIONS Snowy 2.0 road closure

properties.otherAdvice:
The M12 Mwy and associated on- and off-ramps at The Northern Rd (Luddenham), Warami Dr (Badgerys Creek) and Elizabeth Dr (Cecil Hills) are now open.

Visit the project website for some driver-view animations of what to expect as you travel on the new motorway.

Access between the M7 and M12 motorways will be via Elizabeth Dr, until the M7-M12 Interchange opens mid-2026.

Special event clearways will be in place on both sides of Clarence St to facilitate Sydney Trains trackwork replacement bus operations.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully, as vehicles left in a clearway will be towed, and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My CarÂ website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.
properties.displayName: SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS Sydney Trains trackwork

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip on public transport.
properties.otherAdvice:
Temporary traffic changes around the University of Wollongong and TAFE NSW will be in place from Monday 2 March to Friday 10 April, to minimise congestion for the start of the university year.Â 

The temporary traffic changes are as follows:

  • Traffic exiting the M1 Motorway southbound at University Ave, will be diverted left to make a U-turn at the eastern roundabout. University-bound motorists will then be detoured to the Western access route via Murphys Avenue and Robsons Road.Â 
  • Motorists wishing to access the M1 northbound from Keiraville and Gwynneville are encouraged to do so from Robsons Road at West Wollongong to avoid delays.

Bus access from University Avenue into Irvine Street will not be affected during diversions. Outside peak periods traffic will continue to access the University via Irvine Street and Northfields Avenue.

Traffic controllers will be on site to direct motorists.
• From Sunday, 29 March, the third lane is open to traffic in this southbound section of the M7 Mwy.
No access to Gore Hill Fwy southbound from the following roads: Longueville Rd, Pacific Hwy, the Lane Cove Tunnel, Reserve Rd, Willoughby Rd, Brook St, Miller St, or Military Rd.
properties.displayName: CHANGED TRAFFIC CONDITIONS Warringah Freeway Upgrade

properties.diversions:
Vehicles wider than 3m: use an alternative route such as the Oxley Hwy (check travel conditions), Gwydir Hwy or New England Hwy.Â 

Unless specified otherwise, operators of Class 1,2 & 3 vehicles travelling under a NoticeÂ must consult their relevant enforceable network map to confirm a suitable approved alternative route.

Operators of Class 1, 2 & 3 vehicles travelling under a PermitÂ must not travel off the approved route listed in the permit unless an updated permit is obtained from the NHVR.

properties.otherAdvice: Reduced speed limit is expected to remain until at least 20 Apr 2026.

webLinks.linkText: Great Western Highway Road Closure at Victoria Pass

properties.headline: EMERGENCY ROADWORK Road damage MOUNT VICTORIA Great Western Highway at Victoria Pass
properties.otherAdvice: The highway is closed between Coxs River Rd and Victoria St. Great Western Hwy is open between Lithgow and Little Hartley.
properties.diversions:
Westbound: Chifley Rd and Darling Causeway.

Eastbound: Harley Ave, Darling Causeway, Chifley Rd.

This will add approximately 25 minutes to your travel time.

Through traffic on Great Western Hwy can consider alternative routes such as the Golden Hwy or Hume Hwy.

  • Detour is suitable for general access vehicles and Performance Based Standards (PBS) Level 1 heavy vehicles up to 20 metres long
  • Restricted access heavy vehicle operators who are unable to travel on an approved Notice Network are advised to reroute their journeys.
  • Restricted access heavy vehicle operators who are unable to travel on a Notice Network areÂ advised to applyÂ for an amended permit via NHVR Go.Â 
  • Please note that Class 1 Oversize and/or Over Mass (OSOM) vehicles and Special Purpose Vehicles (SPV) cannot currently be accommodated on the Chifley Rd/Darling Causeway detour route.Â 
  • To check approved Notice Networks, heavy vehicle operators can use the NHVR National Network Map to find an alternative route for their combination: https://www.nhvr.gov.au/road-access/route-planner/national-network-map

Operators who require a NHVR issued Permit are reminded that they must only travel on the roads approved in their Permit.

properties.otherAdvice:
A reduced load limit on Hampden Bridge has been introduced, effective from Friday 27 June 2025.

Heavy vehicles over 23 tonnes are restricted from using the bridge and will need to plan a suitable detour.

All other vehicles will not be affected and can continue to use the bridge.

Emergency services vehicles over 23 tonnes will still be able to use the bridge at all times.

Heavy Vehicle operators of Class 1,2, and 3 vehicles travelling under notice must consult their relevant enforceable network map to confirm a suitable and approved detour route.Â
